nodes.label: FileTree vs Tree Design Pattern
nodes.rationale: FileTree was designed as a simpler alternative to the verbose Tree component, using indented text syntax (2 spaces = 1 nesting level) to reduce code by ~90%. The design trade-off is that Tree remains for programmatic/dynamic use cases while FileTree handles static documentation structures.

nodes.label: GDPR Records of Processing Activities Pattern
nodes.norm_label: gdpr records of processing activities pattern

nodes.label: Copy-and-Paste Component Philosophy
nodes.rationale: Components are copied directly into the user project rather than installed as a dependency so users own the code and can modify it freely. Zero runtime dependencies — each component is a standalone .tsx file. CSS variables are used instead of hardcoded colors so components follow any Tailwind theme in light and dark mode without extra config.
